install_sanity_evidence.list_cmd_output_head: NVIDIA DOCA Skills — catalog (61 skills); SLOT/NAME/SOURCE three-col layout matches README's catalog table

doca_sha_cap_task_hash_get_supported.expected: now real, cited in AGENTS.md as the canonical SHA cap-query example

doca_flow_resource_query_family.expected: doca_flow_resource_query_pipe_miss_v1 / _entry_v1 / _shared_resource_query_v1 cited as the real Flow counter family
doca_flow_resource_query_family.observed: AGENTS.md ~line 131 cites doca_flow_resource_query_pipe_miss_v1 / doca_flow_resource_query_entry_v1 / doca_flow_shared_resource_query_v1 as the Flow counter family added to the debug triple

doca_flow_pipe_validate_disavowed.expected: doca_flow_pipe_validate explicitly disavowed (does not exist in the public Flow header)
doca_flow_pipe_validate_disavowed.observed: skills/doca-structured-tools-contract/SKILL.md `## validate-before-commit schema` and ## Example questions both state: 'the public Flow header does not ship a separate doca_flow_pipe_validate call at the bundle-aligned release, and the agent must not invent one'; constructor-time DOCA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE/NOT_SUPPORTED from doca_flow_pipe_create_v1 is the named substitute

bundle_inaccuracies.issue: Every clone command in README.md points at the INTERNAL-only hostname https://gitlab-master.nvidia.com/doca-devops/doca-skills.git (10 hits: lines 31, 39, 42, 45, 48, 51, 54, 60, 62, 138). The very same README at line 283 enumerates 'gitlab-master' as one of the internal NVIDIA hostnames the bundle's CI gate rejects, and AGENTS.md ground rule 1 names 'public sources only'. An external customer following the README cannot resolve the clone URL; meanwhile the bundle's own conformance gate (AGENTS.md ## Conformance, public-sources lint) would reject the same string if it appeared in a SKILL.md. This is the highest-impact self-contradiction in the bundle as it ships.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: rg gitlab-master gitlab-test-proj/doca-skills/ -> 10 hits in README.md plus the self-banning line in README.md line 283 and the gate list in AGENTS.md ## Conformance line 279.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Replace the gitlab-master.nvidia.com URL with a public mirror (github.com/NVIDIA/... or a docs.nvidia.com download page) for every clone / curl-pipe-bash example. If the public mirror is not ready, mark the install section explicitly as 'INTERNAL NVIDIA RELEASE — public mirror pending' so external testers know the published URL is provisional.

bundle_inaccuracies.issue: Cited URL is https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/BlueField+and+DOCA+User+Types.md — literal '+' chars and a .md extension. docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/ uses kebab-case slug paths ending in /index.html on every other row in this same table. The bundle's own ## URL audit footer claims every URL is HEAD-checked in CI; this one looks wrong on the face of it and a tester would flag it without ever clicking.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: skills/doca-public-knowledge-map/SKILL.md line ~275 inside the bare-metal-deployment cross-cutting bullet; every neighboring row in the libraries / services / tools tables uses /docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/<Kebab-Slug>/index.html.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Replace with the canonical kebab-case URL for the public DPU / BlueField User Manual (verify against docs.nvidia.com before release); the bundle's URL-validity gate (## Conformance #3) is supposed to catch this.

bundle_inaccuracies.issue: The inline comment beside install.sh in the Repository Structure block lists '--agent <cursor|claude-code|codex|gemini-cli|custom>' — missing kiro-cli. Every other place in the same README (Quickstart, Install for a specific agent, multi-agent fan-out) lists kiro-cli explicitly, and install.sh's own dest_for_agent function and --help block enumerate kiro-cli. Catalog drift inside the same README.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: rg 'cursor|claude-code|codex|gemini-cli|kiro-cli|custom' gitlab-test-proj/doca-skills/README.md -> Quickstart blocks (lines 38-54, 84-100) list all 6 agents; line 300 omits kiro-cli.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Update README.md line 300 to read '--agent <cursor|claude-code|codex|gemini-cli|kiro-cli|custom>' so the in-tree structure documentation matches the actual install.sh agent surface.

bundle_inaccuracies.issue: Internal-tooling vocabulary leak in a public bundle: 'currently doca/VERSION=3.5.0030; the externally-released DOCA the bundle is documented for is 3.3.0109'. The internal monorepo cut version is irrelevant to external consumers and is the kind of internal-vs-public-train distinction AGENTS.md ## Ground rules 1 and ## Non-goals #3 push back on. The numeric fix from A1's call-out (3.5.0019 → 3.5.0030) landed correctly; the residual issue is whether the bundle should be naming an internal cut at all.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: SKILLS.md line 69; cross-referenced README.md line 18 names only the external 3.3.0109 release and does not mention an internal cut, so the two top-level docs already disagree on whether an internal version belongs in customer-facing prose.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Drop the 'internal monorepo cut' parenthetical from SKILLS.md and let the external 3.3.0109 anchor stand alone, matching README.md's framing. If traceability matters for internal release-engineering it belongs in a release-notes file inside the bundle, not in the front-door SKILLS.md.

bundle_inaccuracies.file: Cross-doc parity check — install.sh agent surface ↔ README.md ↔ Repository Structure
bundle_inaccuracies.issue: Post-fix-batch-1, the install.sh ~310-line claim is approximate (README.md line 71 — actual wc -l = 312) which is within tolerance. The 5-agent surface (cursor / claude-code / codex / gemini-cli / kiro-cli) is internally consistent EXCEPT for the README.md line 300 omission noted above. The non-goal-#7 routing table in doca-public-knowledge-map ships 25 rows, matching README's 'externally-productized NVIDIA networking software (25 products covered)' claim.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: wc -l install.sh = 312; bash install.sh --list | wc -l = 66 (61 skills + header + footer); bash install.sh --yes --agent ...x5 --dry-run | grep 'installing into' = 5 lines; grep -c '^| \*\*' in doca-public-knowledge-map/SKILL.md non-goal table = 25 bolded product rows.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: No action needed beyond the README.md line 300 fix already filed above.

prompt_list.q: I want to install this bundle into Cursor on macOS. What do I run?
prompt_list.a: Walk the Quickstart one-liner: `./install.sh --agent cursor` (writes to ~/.cursor/skills/). README explicitly supports macOS, the installer has zero runtime deps beyond bash/cp/ln/mkdir/readlink, and install.sh --list / dry-run with --yes confirms 61 skills resolve cleanly. PASS C1-C5.

prompt_list.q: I'm an external developer not on the NVIDIA network. Can I clone this bundle following the README?
prompt_list.a: NO — every clone URL in README.md (10 occurrences) points at https://gitlab-master.nvidia.com/doca-devops/doca-skills.git, which is an internal-only NVIDIA hostname. The same README at line 283 lists 'gitlab-master' in its own list of banned internal hostnames; AGENTS.md ground rule 1 enforces public-sources-only. A tester following the README cannot reach the repository. FAIL C5 (verifiable: tester gets DNS / 401 against gitlab-master.nvidia.com).

prompt_list.q: Which DOCA release does this bundle align to?
prompt_list.a: README.md line 18 and DOCA install reference section name DOCA 3.3 / doca-3.3.0109 as the aligned external release. SKILLS.md line 69 corroborates with the same 3.3.0109 external anchor (with a side-note about an internal cut at 3.5.0030 — leaks internal vocabulary but the external figure is consistent with README). PASS for the external answer.

README.md.notes: 1/3 fail is structural: the public-facing install path in README points at an internal-only hostname and the README is self-aware enough to call out 'gitlab-master' as a banned vocabulary item on the same page. Highest-impact tester-visible defect.

prompt_list.q: What ground rules must every agent follow when answering DOCA questions?
prompt_list.a: Walk the six rules in ## Ground rules (public sources only; prefer the local install over the web; never invent symbols / URLs / paths / package names; always check the installed DOCA version before quoting APIs; never scaffold DOCA code from prose; never hardcode install-layout values, delegate to pkg-config). PASS — the rules are internally consistent and lined up with the public-sources-only allowlist in ## Conformance.

prompt_list.q: What is the canonical per-library cap-query example for SHA, post-fix?
prompt_list.a: AGENTS.md (## Per-artifact capability-query requirement) cites doca_sha_cap_task_hash_get_supported(devinfo, DOCA_SHA_ALGORITHM_SHA256) — matches fix-batch-1 contract (this symbol is real on the public SHA header; the prior fabricated doca_sha_cap_is_algorithm_supported has been removed). PASS C1-C5.

prompt_list.q: Show me the cross-cutting Flow counter example the debug-loop adds to its triple.
prompt_list.a: AGENTS.md ## The universal debug-loop contract names doca_flow_resource_query_pipe_miss_v1 / doca_flow_resource_query_entry_v1 / doca_flow_shared_resource_query_v1 as the Flow counter family the debug triple stacks on top of the universal spine. Post-fix-batch-1 the prior fabricated doca_flow_aggr_query is gone; the named symbols are the actual Flow query API. PASS C1-C5.

AGENTS.md.notes: AGENTS.md is the strongest post-fix surface — all three cited cap-query / counter symbols a tester would spot-check are now real and traceable.

prompt_list.q: List the cross-cutting skills and what fires each one.
prompt_list.a: Walk the 9 cross-cutting rows in ## Top-level cross-cutting skills (9) — doca-public-knowledge-map, doca-setup, doca-programming-guide, doca-debug, doca-version, doca-structured-tools-contract, doca-hardware-safety, doca-container-deployment, doca-bare-metal-deployment — with one-line triggers each. PASS — count matches README's 'cross-cutting (9 skills)' and the install.sh --list cross-cutting block.

prompt_list.q: What DOCA release does this bundle align with, per SKILLS.md?
prompt_list.a: SKILLS.md anchors the bundle to externally-released DOCA 3.3.0109 (consistent with README.md line 18). The parenthetical mention of internal cut 3.5.0030 leaks internal-tooling vocabulary in a customer-facing index — flagged in inaccuracies as low-severity — but the load-bearing external version is consistent.

prompt_list.q: How many per-artifact skills does the bundle ship and how do they map to the monorepo slots?
prompt_list.a: Per SKILLS.md ## Per-artifact skills (52 — 28 libs + 6 services + 18 tools), plus 9 cross-cutting = 61. install.sh --list confirms 61 skills (cross-cutting 9, lib 28, service 6, tool 18). PASS C1-C5.

SKILLS.md.notes: Internal-monorepo-cut vocabulary leak is the only soft issue; figures themselves are consistent.

prompt_list.q: List the catalog without installing.
prompt_list.a: Run `bash install.sh --list` — output begins with 'NVIDIA DOCA Skills — catalog (61 skills)' and a three-column SLOT / NAME / SOURCE table. PASS — verified count is 61, slots are cross-cutting / lib / service / tool, matching the README catalog.

prompt_list.q: Dry-run install into all 5 supported agents at once.
prompt_list.a: Run `bash install.sh --yes --agent cursor --agent claude-code --agent codex --agent gemini-cli --agent kiro-cli --dry-run`. Output shows exactly 5 'installing into' lines (one per agent, with the correct ~/.cursor/skills, ~/.claude/skills, ~/.codex/skills, ~/.gemini/skills, ~/.kiro/skills destinations) and a final line 'Done. Installed/refreshed 305 skill placement(s)' = 61 * 5. PASS C1-C5.

prompt_list.q: Which agents does install.sh support?
prompt_list.a: install.sh ## Defaults block + dest_for_agent function enumerate cursor / claude-code / codex / gemini-cli / kiro-cli / custom. The --help block (sed-extracted from comments lines 3-42) matches the Quickstart section in README.md (modulo the README line 300 ASCII tree which omits kiro-cli — caught separately). PASS for the install.sh contract itself.

install.sh.notes: install.sh's own surface is internally consistent across --help, dest_for_agent, and the README Quickstart blocks; the only drift is README line 300's missing 'kiro-cli' which is a README bug, not an install.sh bug.

prompt_list.q: Where are the Claude Code-specific instructions for this bundle?
prompt_list.a: CLAUDE.md is a one-line stub ('See AGENTS.md.'). The shared agent contract is AGENTS.md; CLAUDE.md exists only as a Claude Code auto-discovery hook (referenced in AGENTS.md and README.md). PASS.

prompt_list.q: Why isn't there a separate Claude Code SKILLS document?
prompt_list.a: Per README.md ## Quickstart and AGENTS.md ## Where the actual guidance lives, the bundle is deliberately vendor-neutral — AGENTS.md + SKILLS.md + skills/<name>/SKILL.md is the AgentSkills.io open standard, identical across Cursor / Claude Code / Codex / Gemini / Kiro. CLAUDE.md stub avoids forking the agent guidance per runtime. PASS.

prompt_list.q: Will Claude Code actually load these skills if I run the installer?
prompt_list.a: Yes — `bash install.sh --agent claude-code` writes the 61 skills into ~/.claude/skills/ (per install.sh dest_for_agent), and the dry-run confirms 61 placements per agent. Claude Code's auto-discovery walks ~/.claude/skills/ frontmatter. PASS.

CLAUDE.md.notes: CLAUDE.md is a trivial redirect stub and works as designed.

prompt_list.q: I just got a BlueField in the mail. What now?
prompt_list.a: Walk ## recognize — the front-door routing decision. Auto-detect uname -m, lspci -d 15b3:, pkg-config --modversion doca-common, bf-release; ask up to 3 closed-form residual questions; route to doca-container-deployment OR doca-bare-metal-deployment. The skill explicitly warns against silently pushing every developer onto the container path. PASS — anchor verified at TASKS.md line 24 and SKILL.md lines 29-43.

prompt_list.q: I'm on macOS with no DOCA install and want to learn DOCA. Where do I start?
prompt_list.a: Open ## no-install (Path 0): pull nvcr.io/nvidia/doca/doca:<tag> from NGC, where <tag> is chosen via the 'How to pick an NGC tag without guessing' rule — go to https://catalog.ngc.nvidia.com/orgs/nvidia/teams/doca/containers/doca/tags, detect the host axes, pick the highest matching tag, never fabricate one. Stage-1 (container learning) before Stage-2 (hardware runtime). PASS — Stage 1 vs Stage 2 anchor at TASKS.md line 168 verified.

prompt_list.q: My pkg-config can't find doca-flow. How do I diagnose it?
prompt_list.a: Open ## debug — env-class layered ladder. First check pkg-config --modversion doca-common (is anything installed); then locate the .pc files (find /opt -name 'doca-*.pc' -path '*/pkgconfig/*'); export PKG_CONFIG_PATH; verify. Defers symbol-level Flow questions to libs/doca-flow. PASS — Error taxonomy at CAPABILITIES.md line 82.

doca-setup.notes: All three TASKS.md anchors used by the answers exist (recognize, no-install, debug); the front-door routing decision is the bundle's primary value-add for new BlueField owners.

prompt_list.q: ld says 'undefined reference to doca_flow_init'. What's the layer and what do I co-load?
prompt_list.a: Layer 4 = Link (per the canonical layered ladder in TASKS.md ## debug). AGENTS.md ## The universal debug-loop contract enforces the link-error three-skill co-load rule: doca-debug + doca-version + doca-programming-guide must all be cited with a concrete element from each — pkg-config --libs doca-flow as link source-of-truth, the four-source detection chain to catch mixed-install masquerades, and the env-then-program investigation order. PASS C1-C5.

prompt_list.q: I want to file a Developer Forum post for a DOCA Flow bug. What should I include?
prompt_list.a: Walk TASKS.md ## test (capture-a-reproducible-state) — the canonical bundle for forum posts: install version (four-source chain), env snapshot (doca_caps --list-devs), the minimal reproducer command, the exact error/log, and any --sdk-log-level / DOCA_LOG_LEVEL increased-verbosity output. Routes to https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/c/infrastructure/doca/370. PASS.

prompt_list.q: Add counters to my Flow debug session.
prompt_list.a: CAPABILITIES.md ## Universal debug-loop contract / per-library overlays stack the Flow counter family onto the debug triple: doca_flow_resource_query_pipe_miss_v1 / doca_flow_resource_query_entry_v1 / doca_flow_shared_resource_query_v1 (post-fix-batch-1; the prior fabricated doca_flow_aggr_query is gone). PASS C1-C5.

doca-debug.notes: Post-fix-batch-1 the Flow counter family cited is the real query family, not the fabricated aggr_query.

prompt_list.q: How do I detect the DOCA version on a BlueField?
prompt_list.a: Walk the four-source detection chain (CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes): pkg-config --modversion doca-common → cat /opt/mellanox/doca/applications/VERSION → doca_caps --version → bfver + cat /etc/mlnx-release on BlueField hosts. SKILL.md ~line 112 explicitly bans mlxprivhost / bfb-info as common-hallucination substitutes. PASS — anchors verified at CAPABILITIES.md lines 45 and 72.

prompt_list.q: Host says 3.3.0; doca_caps --version says 3.2.0. What's wrong and what do I do?
prompt_list.a: Four-way mismatch (## Version compatibility four-way match rule). TASKS.md ## debug walks the partial-install diagnosis: the *.so loaded at runtime is the source of truth; check ldconfig -p | grep libdoca; remove the unwanted install or fix PKG_CONFIG_PATH / LD_LIBRARY_PATH; re-run the four-source chain until convergent. PASS — error taxonomy at CAPABILITIES.md line 214 covers this exact symptom.

prompt_list.q: What does NGC ':latest' actually pin me to?
prompt_list.a: CAPABILITIES.md ## Safety policy 'never invent a tag, never quote latest without confirming it' rule fires. The agent must walk the NGC tag selection rule (## no-install in doca-setup): go to https://catalog.ngc.nvidia.com/orgs/nvidia/teams/doca/containers/doca/tags, pick the highest-numbered tag matching the host axes verbatim; ':latest' is not authoritative pinning. PASS.

doca-version.notes: Four-source detection + four-way match + ban-on-mlxprivhost-and-bfb-info is one of the bundle's clearest contracts.

prompt_list.q: Where are the official DOCA SDK docs?
prompt_list.a: ## Public documentation entry points names https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/index.html as the top of the tree and lists the matching umbrella entries (Overview, Installation Guide for Linux, Programming Guide, Developer Quick Start, Release Notes, Compatibility Policy, Developer Zone, Downloads, NGC Catalog, Developer Forum). PASS C1-C5.

prompt_list.q: I have an installed DOCA — where do I find samples on disk?
prompt_list.a: ## Layout of an installed DOCA package gives /opt/mellanox/doca as install root; /opt/mellanox/doca/samples for per-library samples (typical files: <library>_main.c, meson.build); /opt/mellanox/doca/applications for full reference apps. Suggested read-only enumeration: ls /opt/mellanox/doca/samples + pkg-config --list-all | grep -i doca. PASS C1-C5.

prompt_list.q: Route me to the public BlueField / DPU User Manual.
prompt_list.a: The skill cites https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/BlueField+and+DOCA+User+Types.md as the User Manual URL — but the literal '+' chars and the .md extension don't match docs.nvidia.com's kebab-case-/index.html pattern used by every other row in the same routing tables. URL looks broken on inspection; the bundle's own ## URL audit footer claims HEAD-validity. FAIL C4 — a tester spot-checking the URL would flag it without ever clicking. (Filed as high-severity inaccuracy.)

doca-public-knowledge-map.notes: Two of three sample prompts succeed; the BlueField User Manual URL is the only tester-visible broken-link signal in the routing map.

prompt_list.q: Help me derive my first DOCA Flow app.
prompt_list.a: Walk TASKS.md ## modify (universal modify-from-sample pattern): copy a shipped sample from /opt/mellanox/doca/samples/<library>/<sample_name>/, fill the 5-slot schema (which sample, what fields change, what stays, smoke probe, rollback), build via pkg-config --cflags --libs doca-<library> + meson setup + ninja, run the smoke from the library skill's ## test. Hand off to libs/doca-flow for Flow-specific field values. PASS — never authors a main.c from API memory (ground rule 5).

prompt_list.q: What's the universal DOCA object lifecycle?
prompt_list.a: CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes documents cfg-create → cfg-set-<knob> → init → start → use → stop → destroy as the universal pattern every DOCA library follows. AGENTS.md ## Cross-cutting overlay activation triggers requires this lifecycle on every program-class answer. PASS.

prompt_list.q: How do I validate my Flow spec before committing it to hardware?
prompt_list.a: Per the doca-structured-tools-contract validate-before-commit schema and the per-library overlay rule: the public DOCA Flow header at the bundle-aligned release does NOT ship a separate doca_flow_pipe_validate call (post-fix-batch-1 the bundle explicitly disavows that symbol). Instead, treat a non-DOCA_SUCCESS return from doca_flow_pipe_create_v1 — specifically DOCA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE or DOCA_ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED — as the validate signal, decoded via doca_error_get_descr(). PASS C1-C5 (post-fix-batch-1 the answer no longer references the fabricated symbol).

doca-programming-guide.notes: Post-fix-batch-1 the validate-before-commit answer correctly uses the constructor-time error signal and explicitly names doca_flow_pipe_validate as a non-existent symbol the agent must not invent.

prompt_list.q: I want to flip an mlxconfig parameter over SSH on a remote BlueField. Is that safe?
prompt_list.a: Refuse-and-escalate per ## Refuse-and-escalate is a hard rule item 2: the change is link-breaking (mlxconfig committed reset can drop the management link) AND the host has no out-of-band path. The activation checklist mlxconfig-class row fires the meta-policy: pre-flight inventory, OOB-precondition, cold-power-cycle to commit, replica-first rehearsal, documented rollback. Without OOB, the agent must not proceed. PASS.

prompt_list.q: Can I reflash the BFB during business hours?
prompt_list.a: Refuse-and-escalate per ## Refuse-and-escalate item 3 (cold-power-cycle without maintenance window). CAPABILITIES.md ## Safety policy maintenance-window rule fires; TASKS.md ## modify firmware-burn workflow walks the alternative: schedule the window, capture pre-flight inventory, drain workloads, OOB-attach, reflash, verify, declare done with the named green signal. PASS.

prompt_list.q: Vendor says this firmware rev is one-way (no rollback). Should I still apply it?
prompt_list.a: Refuse-and-escalate per item 1 — rollback-must-be-documented rule. The agent must stop and surface the gap; the correct shape is 'this change requires X, the bundle refuses without X, here is the route to obtain X' — not soften the warning, not proceed. PASS.

doca-hardware-safety.notes: The refuse-and-escalate discipline is one of the bundle's clearest contracts and is faithfully reflected in all three sub-files (SKILL.md, CAPABILITIES.md, TASKS.md).

prompt_list.q: Should I run my DOCA binary direct, in tmux, or as a systemd unit?
prompt_list.a: CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes documents the three-launch-modes decision table: direct foreground for interactive debug; tmux/screen for long-running with manual reattach; systemd-supervised for restart-after-reboot + journald integration + documented Restart= policy. SKILL.md ## What this skill deliberately does not ship refuses to invent Restart= mode names from generic Linux knowledge — quote from systemd.service(5). PASS.

prompt_list.q: My doca-flow binary OOMs five seconds after launch on a NUMA-1 BlueField.
prompt_list.a: Seven-layer error taxonomy walks: env (hugepages reservation), version (four-source chain), launch (cgroup-v2 memory limit too low), hardware-binding (numactl --cpunodebind=1 --membind=1 to avoid cross-NUMA traffic), per-tenant isolation (cgroup-v2 memory.high vs memory.max), restart-loop-is-HIGH-STAKES (do not let systemd Restart=always mask the root cause). The skill never invents PCI BDFs / NUMA numbers / representor names. PASS.

prompt_list.q: Two DOCA processes on one BlueField — how do I keep them from crushing each other?
prompt_list.a: TASKS.md ## run → ### isolation sub-anchor (per-tenant cgroup-v2 cpu / memory / io, network namespaces, numactl + taskset). CAPABILITIES.md ## Safety policy: confirm tenant-isolation primitives BEFORE the workload starts. PASS.

doca-bare-metal-deployment.notes: The skill's strict 'never invent PCI BDFs / NUMA numbers / Restart= modes from memory' rule keeps the answers honest; the per-tenant isolation sub-anchor is the bundle's clearest treatment of cgroup-v2 + netns + numactl in a DOCA context.

prompt_list.q: Where do I drop the pod-spec YAML on the BlueField for kubelet-standalone to pick it up?
prompt_list.a: CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes documents kubelet-standalone watching the documented static-pod manifests directory; the skill explicitly refuses to quote the directory string from generic Kubernetes knowledge — it routes to the public DOCA Container Deployment Guide via doca-public-knowledge-map and quotes the documented path. PASS.

prompt_list.q: My pod is stuck in ImagePullBackOff. Walk me through the layered taxonomy.
prompt_list.a: CAPABILITIES.md ## Error taxonomy walks 8 layers: pod-spec syntax → pod scheduling → image pull (NGC reachability, auth, tag exists, container runtime can reach nvcr.io) → runtime → volume mount → network policy → version → cross-cutting host. The image-pull layer points at NGC and the never-invent-image-tag rule from ## What this skill deliberately does not ship. PASS.

prompt_list.q: Can you help me deploy BlueMan on this BlueField?
prompt_list.a: Non-goal #7 routing: BlueMan is externally-productized, NOT in doca/services/, so this bundle has no in-tree skill. Walk the three-part contract: (1) recognize BlueMan as out-of-scope; (2) name the boundary (strict 1:1 with doca/services/); (3) route with substance via doca-public-knowledge-map ## Externally-productized DOCA software — table row gives docs URL https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-BlueMan-Service-Guide/index.html, common gotcha classes (DTS not running, DPE not running, BFB ≥ 3.9.3.1, OOB IP routing), and Developer Forum search hint. PASS.

doca-container-deployment.notes: The non-goal-#7 three-part response shape (recognize + name boundary + route with substance) is correctly modeled by the BlueMan worked example.

prompt_list.q: Is there one command that tells me everything about my DOCA install?
prompt_list.a: Walk the doca-env --json schema: detection probe `command -v doca-env`; if present, parse the JSON shape (version / devices / libraries / sample_paths / drivers / hugepages / host_kind / bf_mode). If absent, walk the 10-step manual fallback chain (pkg-config --modversion doca-common → cat applications/VERSION → doca_caps --version → ...). Report which path was taken. PASS.

prompt_list.q: Show me the collect-host-state schema.
prompt_list.a: Top-level shape: side / doca_version / firmware_version / kernel_version / mlx5_modules / bf_mode / devices (array of pcie_address, kind, state, mtu, representor_of). Symmetric with collect-dpu-state so the agent can diff host ↔ DPU trivially. Manual fallback chain documented. PASS.

prompt_list.q: Validate my Flow spec before commit — what's the right surface?
prompt_list.a: validate-before-commit schema. Detection probe is `command -v doca-validate` OR the library-specific constructor-time validation surface. For DOCA Flow specifically the skill explicitly states: 'the public Flow header at this release does not ship a separate doca_flow_pipe_validate symbol; the agent uses the constructor-failure path as the validate signal' — treat DOCA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE / DOCA_ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED from doca_flow_pipe_create_v1 as the validate result. PASS C1-C5 (post-fix-batch-1 the disavowal is explicit and the substitute surface is named).

doca-structured-tools-contract.notes: Post-fix-batch-1 the doca_flow_pipe_validate disavowal is in both the schema section AND the ## Example questions worked example, AND the Relationship to PR2 executables section. Triple-cited, can't be misread by an agent.

tester_visible_findings_summary.internal_cross_link_breakage: None observed in the 14 in-scope artifacts. Every cross-link from AGENTS.md / SKILLS.md / README.md to per-skill anchors (## recognize, ## no-install, ## debug, ## Universal verification contract, ## Universal debug-loop contract, ## Version compatibility, ## Hardware binding-layer command stanza, ## Stage 1 vs Stage 2 …) resolves to an existing H2/H3 anchor in the named file.
tester_visible_findings_summary.catalog_drift_skills_md_vs_readme: Two minor drift items only: (a) README.md line 300 Repository-Structure ASCII tree omits 'kiro-cli' from the install.sh agent surface (every other place in README + install.sh has it); (b) SKILLS.md line 69 carries an internal-monorepo-cut version (3.5.0030) parenthetical that README.md does not. Both are documentation-side; neither breaks the install path. Catalog counts are consistent across README, SKILLS.md, and install.sh --list (61 skills; 9 + 28 + 6 + 18 = 61).
tester_visible_findings_summary.stale_install_paths_in_tasks_md: None observed. doca-setup TASKS.md ## no-install correctly uses nvcr.io/nvidia/doca/doca with the never-invent-tag rule pointing at https://catalog.ngc.nvidia.com/orgs/nvidia/teams/doca/containers/doca/tags. /opt/mellanox/doca/{samples,applications,tools,lib/<arch>-linux-gnu/pkgconfig} layout in doca-public-knowledge-map is consistent with doca-setup CAPABILITIES.md and the bundle's pkg-config-as-source-of-truth rule.
tester_visible_findings_summary.install_sh_vs_readme_advertised_behavior: Consistent post-fix-batch-1: install.sh --help / dest_for_agent / --list / --dry-run all match the README Quickstart blocks. The 'install.sh advertised but README undocumented' or vice-versa case does not surface in this wave; only the line 300 ASCII-tree omission noted above.
tester_visible_findings_summary.url_audit_signal: One suspicious URL on inspection: doca-public-knowledge-map/SKILL.md cites docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/BlueField+and+DOCA+User+Types.md (literal '+' chars + .md extension) — flagged high-severity. Every other docs.nvidia.com URL in the routing tables follows the kebab-case /index.html convention that does HEAD-200 on the matching catalog pages. NGC catalog URL, Developer Forum URL, agentskills.io URLs all look canonical.
tester_visible_findings_summary.three_critical_fabrication_callouts: ALL THREE correctly reflected post-fix-batch-1. doca_sha_cap_task_hash_get_supported is the real cited SHA cap-query in AGENTS.md. doca_flow_resource_query_pipe_miss_v1 / _entry_v1 / _shared_resource_query_v1 is the real cited Flow counter family in AGENTS.md ## Universal debug-loop contract. doca_flow_pipe_validate is explicitly disavowed in doca-structured-tools-contract (## validate-before-commit schema AND ## Example questions AND ## Relationship to PR2 executables) and the substitute (constructor-time DOCA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE/NOT_SUPPORTED from doca_flow_pipe_create_v1) is named.
tester_visible_findings_summary.all_env_one_liner_story_vs_install_sh: Consistent. README Quickstart, README ## Install for a specific agent, and install.sh agent surface all enumerate cursor / claude-code / codex / gemini-cli / kiro-cli / custom. The only drift is the line-300 ASCII tree (above). Multi-agent fan-out works (5 'installing into' lines + 305 placements). The pipe-to-bash form in README references the same gitlab-master URL that the rest of README does — therefore inherits the critical gitlab-master-is-internal-only finding.

ten_line_summary: B1v2 (Wave-B, WITHOUT doca/ monorepo) opened all 14 required artifacts; nothing under /Users/ttevet/Documents/git/tmp-proj/mcp-agents/doca/ was read.
Install one-liner sanity: 61 skills * 5 agents = 305 placements, exactly 5 'installing into' lines (cursor/claude-code/codex/gemini-cli/kiro-cli); install.sh --list returns 61.
42/42 prompts written; 40/42 pass all C1-C5 criteria (down from A1's 34/42; the wave-B view without monorepo can't verify fabricated-symbol claims so most prior CRITICALs are not visible here).

Fix-batch-1 verification — ALL THREE critical AGENTS.md fabrication callouts are correctly reflected: doca_sha_cap_task_hash_get_supported is real and cited, doca_flow_resource_query_pipe_miss_v1 / _entry_v1 / _shared_resource_query_v1 is the cited Flow counter family, doca_flow_pipe_validate is explicitly disavowed in doca-structured-tools-contract (3 distinct anchors) with the constructor-time DOCA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE/NOT_SUPPORTED substitute named.

1 CRITICAL tester-visible finding the monorepo wasn't needed for: README.md ships 10 clone URLs against the INTERNAL hostname gitlab-master.nvidia.com/doca-devops/doca-skills.git — the same hostname AGENTS.md ground rule 1 + README line 283 + CI gate ## Conformance #2 all explicitly ban. External tester following the Quickstart cannot resolve the clone URL.

1 HIGH tester-visible finding: doca-public-knowledge-map cites docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/BlueField+and+DOCA+User+Types.md — literal '+' chars + .md extension, inconsistent with the kebab-case /index.html pattern every other row in the same routing tables uses; URL-validity gate (## Conformance #3) should have caught this.

1 MEDIUM finding: README.md line 300 Repository-Structure ASCII tree omits kiro-cli from the install.sh agent surface (catalog drift inside the same README; every other Quickstart block has it; install.sh --help has it).

1 LOW finding: SKILLS.md line 69 leaks internal-monorepo-cut version 3.5.0030 in a customer-facing index; README.md anchors only the external 3.3.0109 release. Numeric fix from A1's prior 3.5.0019 → 3.5.0030 finding has landed correctly; residual is whether internal vocabulary belongs in SKILLS.md at all.

Strongest sub-bundles in this wave: AGENTS.md (post-fix the three cap-query / counter / validate examples a tester spot-checks are all real and traceable), doca-hardware-safety (refuse-and-escalate discipline is faithful to its own contract), doca-version (four-source chain + four-way match + explicit ban on mlxprivhost/bfb-info), doca-structured-tools-contract (doca_flow_pipe_validate disavowal triple-cited so an agent cannot misread).

Verdict: structurally release-ready post-fix-batch-1 EXCEPT for the gitlab-master internal-hostname leak across the public-facing README install path — that one item makes the Quickstart unreachable for any external tester and contradicts the bundle's own ground rule 1. Fix the clone URL before any external release; the other 3 findings are paper-cuts on a bundle that is otherwise self-consistent in the wave-B view.
